evidence: Brazil's opening draw made the Haiti match more important for restoring momentum and goal difference. The later 3-0 win directly answered the pressure created by the draw.
Brazil's recovery win kept them in contention and set up the final group fixture against Scotland as the decider for top spot.
Argentina's first clean win reduced pressure and allowed a controlled second performance. The Austria win built on the stability created against Algeria.
Two clean wins put Argentina in command of Group J, turning the final Jordan fixture into a chance to seal top spot and rotate ahead of the knockouts.
A 7-1 result raised expectations and made the following Ivory Coast match the real test of whether Germany's form was genuine or flattered by a weak opener.
Canada's heavy win raised their perceived ceiling and set up the Switzerland fixture as the first real test of their dark-horse credibility against seeded opposition.
Japan's dominant 4-0 win strengthened their tournament narrative and made the Sweden fixture a measure of their true ceiling against a traditional power.
Portugal's opening draw raised the stakes of the Colombia head-to-head because they could no longer rely on a perfect start to control the group.
Colombia's win created a direct table contrast with Portugal's draw, turning their head-to-head into a contest for group control rather than routine qualification.
